title: She Used To Be My Girl

jokes.text: It helps if you wrap it in a slice of cheese.
jokes.setup: Dog won't take medicine, Mom offers helpful suggestion
jokes.punchline: Sets up Homer's immediate cheese grab
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for Homer's predictable but effective cheese grab

jokes.text: Free cheese! / Homer, that was for the dog.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests wrapping dog pill in cheese
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately grabs it as 'free cheese'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - pure selfishness disguised as enthusiasm

jokes.text: What's happened to us, Marge? We used to feed each other cheese and laugh all night.
jokes.setup: Homer reflects on their relationship after eating dog's cheese
jokes.punchline: Romanticizes feeding each other cheese as peak romance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - turning cheese-eating into romantic nostalgia

jokes.text: Then came the heart attacks.
jokes.setup: Homer romanticizes their cheese-eating past
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact explanation for why they stopped
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery destroys Homer's romantic nostalgia with harsh reality

jokes.text: The circus! / A media circus. / I don't know the difference!
jokes.setup: Someone mentions media circus
jokes.punchline: Homer gets excited thinking it's an actual circus, then admits confusion
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer misunderstanding, but executed efficiently

jokes.text: ABC, CBS... the Christian Science Monitor has a flat tire. / I'll get the spare. / No. We must wait for God to jack us.
jokes.setup: Christian Science Monitor reporter has flat tire
jokes.punchline: Must wait for God to jack the car (religious devotion taken literally)
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of Christian Science beliefs applied to car repair

jokes.text: Kent, I'm hiding nothing... Except this puppy.
jokes.setup: Mayor denies hiding anything when accused of scandal
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he's hiding a puppy
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - undermines his denial with innocent admission

jokes.text: Look into his eyes and tell me I'm lying. / Well, I'm placated, and so are all my friends in the local media.
jokes.setup: Mayor uses puppy to deflect serious questions
jokes.punchline: Kent and local media are immediately satisfied by cute puppy
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how easily media can be distracted from real issues

jokes.text: Hey, Kent, run over any more pedestrians? / Those records are sealed.
jokes.setup: Chloe greets Kent with apparent inside knowledge
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Kent hitting pedestrians, his defensive response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Kent's character flaws, delivered casually

jokes.text: I didn't think anyone successful came from Springfield. / What about that two-headed goat? / Technically, he was born in Shelbyville. / Yes, but he came here to die.
jokes.setup: Discussion of successful people from Springfield
jokes.punchline: Two-headed goat's claim to fame is coming to Springfield to die
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that makes Springfield even more pathetic

jokes.text: One of you ate tin cans, one of you ate health food. How you solved crimes, I'll never know.
jokes.setup: Reference to two-headed goat
jokes.punchline: Goat somehow solved crimes despite bizarre diet differences
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd concept of crime-solving two-headed goat with dietary conflicts

jokes.text: You're the major? / That's mayor, you fertile moron.
jokes.setup: Woman misreads Quimby's sash during intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Quimby corrects her while insulting her fertility (ironically)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Quimby moment - arrogant correction mixed with ironic insult given his scandal

jokes.text: Channel Six News rocks. A car chase every night, or the weather girl wears a tube top. / And if she doesn't, you win a pizza.
jokes.setup: Kent defends local news quality
jokes.punchline: Their standards are car chases and tube tops, with pizza prizes
jokes.explanation: Good satire of sensationalist local news priorities

jokes.text: You made a chore wheel that's both fair and wise.
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to console Marge about her achievements
jokes.punchline: Chore wheel is presented as major accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Lisa's earnest attempt to find something impressive about Marge's domestic life

jokes.text: Mayor Quimby is my father. Give me one million dollars.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to get in on Quimby lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Bart's direct, shameless demand for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - cuts through complexity with direct greed

jokes.text: Only if it is submitted in writing. / Checkmate.
jokes.setup: Reporter asks if mayor will answer question
jokes.punchline: Mayor's bureaucratic deflection presented as chess victory
jokes.explanation: Quimby's arrogant satisfaction with basic bureaucratic stonewalling

jokes.text: Paris? Just like you always dreamed. / Terrace. Evergreen Terrace. The street that smells like pee.
jokes.setup: Chloe thinks Marge moved to Paris
jokes.punchline: It's just Evergreen Terrace, which smells like pee
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection followed by pathetic reality of Marge's life

jokes.text: Hey, only the lame-os stay. / And your mother.
jokes.setup: Chloe dismisses people who stayed in Springfield
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizes she just insulted Marge
jokes.explanation: Classic foot-in-mouth moment with weak attempted recovery

jokes.text: You insult me, you insult Italy. / Which is shaped like a boot. / Who knew?
jokes.setup: Italian caterer takes offense at food criticism
jokes.punchline: Homer's amazement at Italy being boot-shaped
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer ignorance about basic geography

jokes.text: I remember you from high school. You barfed in my tuba. / I believe it was the school's tuba.
jokes.setup: Someone remembers Homer's high school behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer deflects responsibility by correcting ownership
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - avoiding responsibility through technicality

jokes.text: My back pain's completely cured. / That was one too many.
jokes.setup: Homer compliments dinner enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Goes too far and hurts himself, Marge's dry observation
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical over-enthusiasm leading to self-injury

jokes.text: We finally found out why the dog was scooting around on his butt all day. / Turns out he had an impacted anal gland.
jokes.setup: Chloe asks what's exciting in Marge's life
jokes.punchline: Marge shares grossly inappropriate dinner conversation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Marge's social obliviousness about appropriate topics

jokes.text: So Bob, what religion are you converting to now? / Well, I'll tell you one thing... shalom.
jokes.setup: Chloe interviews Bob Dylan about religious conversions
jokes.punchline: Dylan's vague response with Hebrew greeting
jokes.explanation: Good satire of Dylan's reputation for constant religious changes

jokes.text: I thought he was dead. / Yeah? I guess you ain't been to too many supermarket openings lately.
jokes.setup: Chloe surprised Krusty is alive
jokes.punchline: Krusty's career reduced to supermarket openings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty moment - acknowledging his career decline with bitter humor

jokes.text: The only reason we don't move out of this dunghill is because of my court-ordered ankle bracelet. / I'm here! I'm here! Quit buggin' me.
jokes.setup: Snake explains why he stays in Springfield
jokes.punchline: Ankle bracelet starts beeping and talking to him
jokes.explanation: Good Snake moment with technological absurdity of talking ankle monitor

jokes.text: This is the dance the Chinese government makes dissidents perform before they shoot them.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently performing some kind of dance
jokes.punchline: Claims it's Chinese execution ritual
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor typical of Homer's random cultural 'knowledge'

jokes.text: I swallowed a chicken bone.
jokes.setup: Chloe asks what Homer is doing
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of potentially dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual approach to medical emergencies

jokes.text: Did you just swallow another bone? / Same one.
jokes.setup: Homer appears to have swallowed another bone
jokes.punchline: It's the same bone, implying it came back up
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words create disturbing implication about Homer's digestive system

jokes.text: It's like Christmas in Aspen.
jokes.setup: Homer smells Chloe's serving bowl
jokes.punchline: Overly romantic description of bowl's scent
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to romanticize mundane objects with specific imagery

jokes.text: I'll teach you to give me my first job out of prison.
jokes.setup: Flashback to cafeteria worker spitting in soup
jokes.punchline: Reveals this was Moe's first job after prison
jokes.explanation: Good Moe character development - explains his attitude and criminal background

jokes.text: In this box marked 'honoree,' fill in your names. And over here, write in what you did. I'll be back.
jokes.setup: Presenting awards for journalism achievement
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'awards' are blank forms they have to fill out themselves
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of meaningless recognition. The DIY nature of the 'award' deflates any sense of accomplishment perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, that Moe.
jokes.setup: Moe threatens revenge after being fired
jokes.punchline: Marge's fond, dismissive reaction to criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Marge's typical Minnesota-nice response to criminal threats

jokes.text: I love you, Chloe... and I love you too, brake fluid.
jokes.setup: Young Barney professes love to Chloe
jokes.punchline: Also declares love for brake fluid (his drinking problem)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - romantic declaration undercut by alcoholism, explains Chloe leaving

jokes.text: Why would Homer write his name in grime?
jokes.setup: Bart apparently writing something inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Marge assumes it must be Homer's doing due to childishness
jokes.explanation: Marge's logic about Homer's maturity level relative to Bart

jokes.text: Good news, Marge! I've learned to walk naked on stilts!
jokes.setup: Homer announces good news
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and inappropriate skill he's learned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity - combines nudity, circus skills, and proud announcement of useless achievement

jokes.text: You want your mic back, local anchor? / This just in... I'm pissed off!
jokes.setup: National anchors mock Kent as local anchor
jokes.punchline: Kent's professional news delivery of personal anger
jokes.explanation: Good Kent moment - maintains news delivery format while expressing genuine emotion

jokes.text: You want your mic back, local anchor? This just in... I'm pissed off!
jokes.setup: Kent being mocked by national reporters
jokes.punchline: His angry response uses news anchor format to express personal rage
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Kent can't break from news format even when angry. The professional delivery of personal rage is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You're like the electrical tape that holds the two halves of my car together.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to compliment Marge as family backbone
jokes.punchline: Compares her to electrical tape holding his broken car together
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - attempting sweetness but revealing pathetic state of his possessions

jokes.text: You know what would be a good name for Maggie? Chloe!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to cheer up Marge
jokes.punchline: Suggests naming baby after the woman making Marge insecure
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete obliviousness to why this would upset Marge

jokes.text: You're like the electrical tape that holds the two halves of my car together.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to give Marge romantic compliment
jokes.punchline: Compares her to automotive repair material holding together broken car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer romantic gesture - sincere but completely tone-deaf. The metaphor reveals both his dependence on Marge and his inability to express it properly.

jokes.text: Morley Safer has the biggest head I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Chloe recounts exciting night with celebrities
jokes.punchline: Random physical observation about news anchor
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity observation humor, somewhat random

jokes.text: I mean the lava part, not saving Lisa. / Pretend to care!
jokes.setup: Bart wants to be like Marge
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he wants the dangerous part, not the heroic part
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - wants the exciting danger but not the responsibility

jokes.text: She's glaring at us from the front lawn.
jokes.setup: Lisa needs to ask permission to go with Chloe
jokes.punchline: Marge is already outside angrily watching them
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup for confrontation. Marge's overprotective behavior and lack of subtlety is in character.

jokes.text: How dare you show my daughter a life of fun and possibility?
jokes.setup: Marge confronts Chloe angrily
jokes.punchline: Angry about positive opportunities being offered
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line revealing Marge's insecurity - she's literally angry about good opportunities

jokes.text: Keep your dish-washing hands off the Armani!
jokes.setup: Marge's jealous confrontation with Chloe
jokes.punchline: Class-based insult about manual labor vs designer fashion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Marge's insecurity into class-consciousness. Shows her awareness of economic differences.

jokes.text: For your information, our electric dishwasher is on the fritz. / Not that you care about the ups and downs of my appliances. Do ya? Huh, do ya?
jokes.setup: Marge defending her dishwashing
jokes.punchline: Aggressive confrontation about appliance sympathy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Marge's breakdown - making appliance troubles into personal attack

jokes.text: I'm going to show you some moves I learned from G. Gordon Liddy! I'm so sick of names!
jokes.setup: Fight escalating between Marge and Chloe
jokes.punchline: Chloe's frustration with constant name-dropping
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with self-aware humor about Chloe's constant celebrity references. Marge's G. Gordon Liddy threat is absurd.

jokes.text: There is no need to fight over me. / No one's fighting over you! / Oh. Well, then, carry on.
jokes.setup: Homer assumes women are fighting over him
jokes.punchline: Deflated when corrected, casually permits their violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ego moment followed by casual acceptance of violence

jokes.text: Sometimes when your mom has half a glass of wine, she goes cuckoo bananas.
jokes.setup: Homer explains Marge's behavior to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Blames minimal alcohol consumption for extreme behavior
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Marge's low alcohol tolerance and Homer's casual description

jokes.text: Thinking is for losers!
jokes.setup: Lisa contemplating disobeying her mother
jokes.punchline: Bart's anti-intellectual advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart philosophy - maximum economy expressing his worldview

jokes.text: I'll give you a first-round bye.
jokes.setup: Tom Brokaw asking Chloe to racquetball tournament
jokes.punchline: Offers unfair advantage to secure her attendance
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom Brokaw as smitten and willing to compromise integrity. Mild humor about celebrity networking.

jokes.text: I know you're mad at me, but I hope you're not mad at Cap'n Crunch.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to make peace with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Treats breakfast cereal as innocent third party
jokes.explanation: Sweet maternal humor - Marge anthropomorphizes cereal to lighten mood. Shows her caring nature and desire to reconcile.

jokes.text: Don't we have a reporter who specializes in natural disasters? Yeah, he's busy covering Julia Roberts' last haircut. But seriously, he's dead.
jokes.setup: Question about having specialist reporter for volcano
jokes.punchline: Misdirection from celebrity coverage to actual death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy misdirection - sets up media priorities joke, reveals actual tragedy. Perfect 'but seriously' structure.

jokes.text: Let's see. I've been to Afghanistan, Serbia, Lebanon... / Okay, okay, I lied. I shoot wedding videos / Just Fort Worth!
jokes.setup: Cameraman claims international experience
jokes.punchline: Admits to lying, deflates to wedding videos in Fort Worth
jokes.explanation: Good deflation from exotic locations to mundane wedding work

jokes.text: I'm growing ashamed of my penis.
jokes.setup: Homer at women's conference
jokes.punchline: Conference making him ashamed of masculinity
jokes.explanation: Homer's blunt response to feminist messaging, crude but character-appropriate

jokes.text: What do women want?!
jokes.setup: Homer staying with women while others rescue Lisa
jokes.punchline: Classic male confusion about women, shouted in desperation
jokes.explanation: Generic male confusion about women. Homer's delivery probably saves it but the joke itself is fairly basic and overused.

jokes.text: Okay. I'll run as fast as I can, but I'm full of potato salad.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to rescue mission
jokes.punchline: Reveals digestive impediment to heroic action
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - willing to help but hampered by food consumption. The specific mention of potato salad makes it funnier.

jokes.text: In fact, I don't even know why I'm here. This lava is not a criminal. It hasn't hurt anybody. / Anybody I know.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum blocking access to volcano
jokes.punchline: Doesn't understand why police are needed, qualifies his safety claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - treating natural disaster like police matter, then casual admission people may have died

jokes.text: Yeah, but I'm a cop first... then a boat enthusiast... amateur historian... yo-yo dieter... then I'm a cop again...
jokes.setup: Homer appeals to Wiggum as fellow father
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's confused priority list ending with being cop again
jokes.explanation: Excellent Wiggum rambling with absurd priority system and circular logic

jokes.text: Actually, when it's underground, lava is called magma. / You're so smart, Chloe! / Walter Cronkite told me the same thing. / Shut up!
jokes.setup: Lisa corrects geological terminology
jokes.punchline: Homer praises Chloe incorrectly, Chloe name-drops, Marge explodes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's confusion, Chloe's ego, and Marge's breaking point

jokes.text: Don't worry. We've got two hours before it burns down to my head.
jokes.setup: Marge's hair is on fire from volcano rescue
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact calculation of how long until serious injury
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - Marge's practical assessment of her burning hair. The specific time estimate makes it funnier.

jokes.text: How 'bout a half hour of pity sex? / Is there any other kind?
jokes.setup: Barney asks for pity sex after rescue
jokes.punchline: Chloe's cynical response about all sex being pity sex
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about relationships with good character voice for both

jokes.text: How 'bout a half hour of pity sex? Is there any other kind?
jokes.setup: Barney offering romantic reconciliation after heroic rescue
jokes.punchline: His pathetic proposition met with equally depressing response
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about failed relationships and low expectations. Both characters reveal their romantic damage in efficient exchange.

jokes.text: I mean the lava part, not saving Lisa. Pretend to care!
jokes.setup: Bart saying he wants to be like Marge
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he wants danger, not heroism; Marge's correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - interested in excitement, not altruism. Marge's exasperated correction shows good parenting instincts.

jokes.text: I mean the lava part, not saving Lisa. / Pretend to care!
jokes.setup: Bart wants to be like heroic Marge
jokes.punchline: Only interested in dangerous volcano part, not heroic rescue
jokes.explanation: Repeated for emphasis - Bart's selfish priorities even when praising others

jokes.text: No!
jokes.setup: Fantasy sequence of Marge as successful journalist
jokes.punchline: Dream scenario involves covering sports disaster
jokes.explanation: Fantasy sequence showing alternative life path. The 'No!' suggests sports disappointment, mild humor from contrast.

jokes.text: Praying to Buddha, Jesus, SpongeBob. There's no time to be picky.
jokes.setup: Lisa praying in dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Includes cartoon character with religious figures, pragmatic approach
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa line mixing her intelligence with desperation, cultural reference works

jokes.text: Perhaps we should help. / Screw her!
jokes.setup: Homer suggests helping Lisa in danger
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate callous rejection
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling rivalry - Bart's instant dismissal of helping Lisa
